name: Lead Gen & AI-Qualified Email Outreach Pipeline

parameters.formDescription: Enter a target city and business niche to scrape leads via Google Maps.

nodes.name: Lead Search Form
nodes.notes: Captures Target City and Niche from the user to kick off the scraping pipeline.

nodes.name: Apify - Google Maps Scraper
nodes.notes: Runs apify/google-maps-scraper with Niche + Target City mapped from the form, and waits for the dataset (Run actor and get dataset = synchronous, blocks until finished).

nodes.name: Batch Businesses (10 at a time)
nodes.notes: Batches the scraped business list 10 at a time to protect downstream APIs (e.g. AI classification, enrichment) from rate limits.

nodes.name: Website Check
nodes.notes: True (output 0) = website field is empty/null -> No Website branch. False (output 1) = website exists -> scrape it with Firecrawl.

nodes.name: No Website Flag
nodes.notes: Branch A: no website on record. Flags classification and bumps the inline tracking score by +3, then passes straight to Groq.

nodes.name: Firecrawl - Scrape Homepage
nodes.notes: Branch B: website exists. Scrapes homepage markdown via Firecrawl /v1/scrape. continueOnFail is enabled so a failed scrape doesn't kill the batch - it flows to 'Prepare Scraped Content' with an error field instead.

nodes.name: Prepare Scraped Content
nodes.notes: Re-merges the original business record (pulled from 'Website Check') with the Firecrawl result. Handles both success and the caught-error case cleanly so Groq always gets a consistent shape.

nodes.name: Groq - AI Qualification
nodes.notes: Sends the merged record (either branch) to Groq's chat completions endpoint in JSON mode. System prompt forces the fit_score/pain_points/industry/estimated_size/outreach_angle schema.

nodes.name: Parse Groq Response
nodes.notes: Parses the JSON string inside Groq's choices[0].message.content and merges it back onto the original business record (pulled from 'Website Check') so downstream nodes have both the scrape/AI results and the raw Google Maps fields in one flat object.

nodes.name: Route by Fit Score
nodes.notes: Rule 1: fit_score < 5 -> Rejected. Rule 2: 5-7 -> Warm Prospects. Rule 3: >=8 -> Hot Prospects (feeds the email phase next).

nodes.name: Format - Rejected Row
nodes.notes: Reshapes the record into exact column names for the 'Rejected' tab.

nodes.name: Append - Rejected
nodes.notes: Appends to the 'Rejected' tab of the Lead Gen Pipeline spreadsheet. Set documentId to your actual sheet before running.

nodes.name: Format - Warm Row
nodes.notes: Reshapes the record into exact column names for the 'Warm Prospects' tab.

nodes.name: Append - Warm Prospects
nodes.notes: Appends to the 'Warm Prospects' tab of the Lead Gen Pipeline spreadsheet. Set documentId to your actual sheet before running.

nodes.name: Format - Hot Row
nodes.notes: Reshapes the record into exact column names for the 'Hot Prospects' tab. Email field is left blank for now - no email-enrichment step exists yet in the pipeline.

nodes.name: Append - Hot Prospects
nodes.notes: Appends to the 'Hot Prospects' tab. This is the branch that should feed into the email outreach phase next.

nodes.name: Read Sent History
nodes.notes: Reads the whole 'Sent History' tab fresh on every loop iteration so dedup stays accurate even across batches within the same run. Runs in parallel off 'Append - Hot Prospects' purely so its data can be referenced by name from 'De-duplication Check'.

nodes.name: De-duplication Check
nodes.notes: For each Hot Prospect, checks its Email/Business Name against the Sent History list (referenced from the sibling 'Read Sent History' node) and flags alreadySent true/false.

nodes.name: Not Already Sent?
nodes.notes: True (output 0) = new prospect, not previously emailed -> continue to Groq Email Writer. False (output 1) = already in Sent History -> terminates here (Duplicate - Skip).

nodes.name: Duplicate - Skip (No Action)
nodes.notes: Terminal node. This business was already contacted per Sent History, so the run ends here for this item - no email is sent, no further Sent History write.

nodes.name: Groq - Email Writer
nodes.notes: Writes a personalized 3-sentence cold email using Business Name, City, Pain Points, and Outreach Angle. JSON mode returns {subject, body} for reliable parsing downstream.

nodes.name: Parse Email Draft
nodes.notes: Parses Groq's {subject, body} JSON out of choices[0].message.content, falling back to a safe generic draft if parsing fails so the run never breaks.

nodes.name: Wait 2 Seconds
nodes.notes: Small delay before each send to keep Gmail sending patterns looking human and reduce spam-filter/rate-limit risk.

nodes.name: Gmail - Send Cold Email
nodes.notes: Sends the AI-drafted cold email to the prospect's address.

nodes.name: Append - Sent History
nodes.notes: On successful send, logs Business Name, Email, sent_at timestamp, and status 'Sent' to the Sent History tab so future runs skip this business.

nodes.name: Format - Send Error Row
nodes.notes: Runs only on the Gmail node's error output. Builds a Rejected-tab row capturing the business + the actual send failure reason.

nodes.name: Append - Send Error to Rejected
nodes.notes: Logs a failed cold-email send into the Rejected tab so it doesn't silently vanish, and won't be retried as a duplicate.

nodes.name: Wait Before Email Writer
nodes.notes: Paces the Hot Prospects email-writing call against Groq's rate limit, same reasoning as the 'Wait' node before the qualification call.
